dayName: First Day at the Crow's Wing

scenarios.title: The Merchant's Lost Wagon
scenarios.target: recover the salt-merchant's wagon from the brigands camped at the Crow's Ford

contributions.line: Marek, the brave soldier, bats away the lock's rusted hinges with his muscular grip, but the stubborn mechanism refuses to yield.
Roselle, the cautious peasant, slips silently past the brigand sentry, her quick steps barely disturbing the underbrush.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Marek's brute strength fails to crack the stubborn lock, while Roselle's stealth keeps her hidden from the sentry, but neither can force the situation entirely alone.

scenarios.title: The Brigand's Tongue
scenarios.target: wring the location of the bandit cache out of the prisoner before dawn

contributions.line: Marek, the soldier, leans in hard, his muscular frame intimidating the trembling prisoner into silence.
Veska, the scholar, catches the subtle shifts in the prisoner's story, spotting the inconsistencies.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Marek's physical pressure and Veska's keen eye break the false calm, forcing the prisoner to reveal the bandit's location before dawn.

scenarios.title: Watchtower at the Pass
scenarios.target: commission a new watchtower at the southern pass before winter closes the road

contributions.line: Veska's meticulous plans for the tower's foundation are hampered by her fatigue, making her overly cautious and slow.
Marek's stubborn oversight is marred by his tiredness, leading to distracted decision-making and a shaky timber frame.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Despite their efforts, the crew's fatigue and pessimism cause the structure to be poorly aligned, risking failure when the melt begins.
